name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is a leading enterprise software company providing a cloud-based platform for digital workflows that automate IT service management, customer service, HR delivery, security operations, and business process automation. The company serves government, financial services, healthcare, and technology sectors globally, and has become the fastest organically growing enterprise software company in history, with ambitions to reach $30B in revenue.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has more than doubled full-year revenues since CEO Bill McDermott joined in late 2019 (from ~$4.5B to $13.3B in 2025), while expanding from IT service management into a comprehensive enterprise workflow platform spanning HR, customer service, security, and now AI-native automation [1]. The Armis acquisition meaningfully expands TAM into security. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist AI is ServiceNow's fastest-selling new product in history, with 350+ net new AI innovations shipped in the GenAI platform release; Q1 2026 saw 16 transactions over $5M in new ACV, up 80% YoY, driven substantially by AI-powered workflow automation [1]. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow is the dominant player in IT service management and is rapidly gaining share across enterprise workflow categories, with subscription revenue of $3,671M in Q1 2026 growing 22% YoY; the company is defining the 'platform of platforms' category for enterprise digital workflows [1]. Pipeline (1/2): The $30B revenue aspiration, Armis integration, and continued Now Assist expansion provide strong pipeline visibility, though specific next-gen product launch dates beyond the current roadmap were not identified. R&D Intensity (1/1): ServiceNow invests heavily in R&D relative to enterprise software peers, with continuous platform innovation and 350+ net new features per major release [1].

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's mission to 'make the world work better for everyone' is specific, thoughtful, and clearly connected to the company's platform strategy — automating workflows that free people from mundane tasks to focus on higher-value work. CEO McDermott consistently references this purpose in strategic decisions and has built the 'DESCO21C' (Defining Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century) identity around it [2]. Community Impact (2/2): ServiceNow has been recognized on Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For and Fortune World's Most Admired Companies lists, and the company's platform directly enables ESG management and sustainability reporting for thousands of enterprises, amplifying community impact through its customers [3]. Employee Retention (2/2): The company's 'WE culture' (hungry and humble) has earned consistent top rankings on Glassdoor Best Places to Work. Employee reviews highlight transparent leadership, clear vision communication, and a strong AI-native learning culture with 25,000+ employees united under a shared mission [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): No significant insider buying data identified; CEO McDermott's substantial equity compensation aligns his interests but routine executive selling is typical for high-growth tech companies [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: ServiceNow's financial profile is exceptional across nearly every metric. Subscription revenue of $3,671M grew 22% YoY (19% constant currency) in Q1 2026, and the company raised full-year subscription revenue guidance to $15.74-15.78B, representing 20.5-21% growth [1]. Forward P/E of 18.9x is remarkably reasonable for a company growing revenue 22% with massive recurring revenue. D/E of 20.7% reflects a clean balance sheet. ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target. PEG ratio of 0.92 is below the 1.0 target, indicating the stock is attractively valued relative to growth [4]. Revenue 5-year CAGR of 22.4% (from $7.2B in 2022 to $13.3B in 2025) demonstrates consistent high-growth execution. Profit margin of 12.6% is the one weaker metric, dragged down by heavy investment in growth and stock-based compensation. Liquidity is excellent at $2.12B daily traded value.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): ServiceNow is PROTECTED from AI disruption because AI is deeply integrated into its platform rather than threatening to displace it. Enterprise workflow automation requires deep integration with existing IT infrastructure, regulatory compliance, and organizational processes that AI tools alone cannot replicate without ServiceNow's platform layer [1]. The company's switching costs are enormous — once workflows are built on the Now Platform, migration is extraordinarily costly. AI chatbots and point solutions cannot replicate the platform-of-platforms approach that spans ITSM, HR, security, and customer service. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist AI is ServiceNow's fastest-selling product ever, with AI driving 80% YoY growth in $5M+ ACV transactions. The company shipped 350+ AI innovations in its latest platform release, and AI is now embedded across every workflow category, generating quantifiable incremental revenue [1].

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under Bill McDermott exemplifies infinite-game thinking in enterprise software. The company's 'DESCO21C' aspiration — to become the Defining Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century — is a cause that by definition can never be fully achieved. McDermott's consistent investment in platform expansion (from ITSM to enterprise-wide workflows), the Armis acquisition to enter security, and the all-in bet on AI-native workflows demonstrate courageous long-term thinking. The 'WE culture' of hungry and humble creates genuine trusting teams.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (3/3): ServiceNow's cause — 'make the world work better for everyone' combined with the DESCO21C aspiration — is both idealistic and validated by execution. The company has consistently expanded its platform's scope for over 20 years, growing from a single ITSM product to an enterprise-wide workflow platform that touches every department. McDermott has held this vision since joining in 2019, and it has shaped every major strategic decision including the expansion into HR, security, customer service, and AI [2]. The cause survived multiple market cycles and continues to drive strategic coherence.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): ServiceNow's culture is consistently recognized as exceptional — Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For, Glassdoor Best Places to Work, and Fortune World's Most Admired Companies [2]. Employee reviews cite transparent leadership, clear vision communication, and an 'AI-native, education-forward' culture. CEO McDermott was named a Top CEO by Glassdoor. The 'WE culture' emphasizes acting together, and 25,000+ employees are united under the DESCO21C dream. This is a high-trust organization where people stay for the mission [2].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): ServiceNow competes against Salesforce, Microsoft, and SAP but frames competition as motivation to expand its platform capabilities rather than a war to win. McDermott's competitive language focuses on ServiceNow being the platform that connects all enterprise workflows — acknowledging that each rival has strengths in individual categories while positioning ServiceNow as the integration layer. This is an infinite competitive mindset focused on self-improvement [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): ServiceNow has evolved significantly from an IT ticketing tool to an enterprise workflow platform, and the pivot to AI-native workflows represents another meaningful strategic evolution. The Armis acquisition ($4.2B) to enter cybersecurity demonstrates willingness to make bold bets [1]. However, the company hasn't abandoned its core business model — it has expanded it. The evolution is impressive but incremental rather than transformative in the Sinek sense of blowing up what's working.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): McDermott's decision to pursue the $4.2B Armis acquisition and raise 2026 subscription revenue guidance despite geopolitical headwinds (Iran war impact on Middle East deals) demonstrates willingness to invest for the long term [1]. The company's all-in bet on AI — embedding 350+ AI features across the platform in a single release — represents a courageous strategic commitment. However, these are growth investments at a time of strength rather than costly sacrifices during adversity.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the fastest organically growing enterprise software company in history, with Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3,671M growing 22% YoY and a raised full-year guidance of $15.74-15.78B [1]. The company's AI-first strategy is delivering tangible results — Now Assist is the fastest-selling product in company history, driving 80% YoY growth in $5M+ ACV deals, and the platform's deep integration across enterprise workflows creates massive switching costs [1].
thesis_continued: The financial profile justifies a premium: forward P/E of 18.9x with 22% revenue growth yields a PEG of 0.92, D/E is just 20.7%, and the Armis acquisition expands TAM into cybersecurity [1][4]. The primary risk is profit margin compression from heavy AI investment and stock-based compensation (12.6% GAAP margin), though this is typical for high-growth enterprise software and should improve with operating leverage. At $30B revenue aspiration, ServiceNow has at least another doubling ahead, making this a core BUY for the IOWN growth portfolio [1].
key_catalysts: Now Assist AI driving 80% YoY growth in $5M+ ACV deals, with 350+ AI innovations shipped and AI embedded across every workflow category — becoming the company's fastest-selling product in history [1]
Armis acquisition integration expands TAM into cybersecurity, and raised 2026 subscription revenue guidance to $15.74-15.78B signals continued execution confidence [1]

Path to $30B+ revenue with ServiceNow's platform-of-platforms strategy — expanding from ITSM into HR, security, customer service, and AI-native workflow automation across every enterprise function [1]
key_risks: GAAP profit margin of 12.6% is thin for a premium-valuation enterprise software company, driven by heavy stock-based compensation and growth investment — any deceleration in revenue growth could trigger a valuation reset [1][4]
Geopolitical headwinds — Iran war-related delays in Middle East deals caused a 75bps subscription revenue growth headwind in Q1 and ServiceNow stock fell 14% on the initial report [1]

Competition from Microsoft (Copilot), Salesforce (Einstein), and SAP intensifies as every enterprise software company embeds AI into workflows, potentially commoditizing ServiceNow's AI differentiation over time [2]
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Financial Results and Earnings Call, April 2026; Fortune — ServiceNow $30B revenue aspiration, May 2026
ServiceNow Executive Leadership — Bill McDermott profile; Glassdoor Top CEO recognition; LEADERS Magazine interview, 2024-2026
ServiceNow ESG Management platform documentation; Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For recognition, 2025-2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW key statistics and financial data, accessed May 2026
